messages.content: what is the rate to get into the PTA program at Pima Medical?

The admission requirements and acceptance rates for the Physical Therapist Assistant (PTA) program at Pima Medical Institute may vary each year. It is best to directly contact Pima Medical Institute for the most accurate and up-to-date information regarding their PTA program admission rates.
answer this Why do you want to be a PTA? Why PTA over PT?

As an AI language model, I don't have personal wants or desires. However, I can provide you with some common reasons why individuals may choose to become a Physical Therapist Assistant (PTA) instead of pursuing the path of a Physical Therapist (PT):

1. Hands-on patient care: PTAs directly work with patients to implement treatment plans under the supervision of a PT. They often provide hands-on care, performing exercises, assisting with mobility, and teaching patients proper techniques.

2. Quicker entry into the workforce: Becoming a PTA typically requires completing a two-year associate degree program, whereas becoming a PT typically involves a longer educational pathway, including a doctoral degree. This shorter education period allows PTAs to enter the workforce sooner.

3. Balance between work and personal life: The role of a PTA can offer a greater work-life balance compared to the demands often associated with being a PT. PTAs often work regular hours in outpatient clinics, hospitals, or rehabilitation centers, while PTs may require additional time for administrative tasks, research, or working in extended hours.

4. Similar job satisfaction: PTAs often experience high job satisfaction since they have the opportunity to make a positive impact on patients' lives. They are involved in helping patients regain mobility, reduce pain, and improve their overall quality of life, similar to PTs.

It's important to note that both PTs and PTAs play vital roles in the field of physical therapy, and the decision to pursue either profession depends on individual interests, career goals, and personal circumstances.

check my grammar:I have always had an interest in exploring body movements when I was a kid, and I found myself really into dance class i was really good at grasping dance movement, and i was also interested in human anatomy , until I had neck pain when I worked as a curriculum designer I started to look into physical therapy, I had a lot of relif from physical therapy , and I really want to become PTA, I think PT is my ultimate goal, entering the field as PTA will help provide me insight and basic knowledge I need to know as a PT so It’ll prepare me to get into a PT program.

I have always had an interest in exploring body movements since I was a kid. I found myself really into dance classes and was naturally good at grasping dance movements. Alongside this, I have always been intrigued by human anatomy.

However, my perspective changed when I experienced neck pain while working as a curriculum designer. This prompted me to delve into the field of physical therapy, where I found significant relief through the treatments I received. It was during this process that I developed a strong desire to become a Physical Therapist Assistant (PTA).

While becoming a PTA is my immediate goal, I see it as a stepping stone towards my ultimate aspiration of becoming a Physical Therapist (PT). By entering the field as a PTA, I firmly believe it will provide me with invaluable insight and essential foundational knowledge necessary to succeed in a PT program. This experience will not only deepen my understanding of patient care but also adequately prepare me for my future endeavor in becoming a PT.

check grammar:I moved to sierra vista, arizona at the beginning of this year from China, I did a lot research and consulted PTA program at Pima in Tucson, but I got a job offer in seattle as a preschool teacher, so I moved to seattle worked as a preschool teacher, but I realize I still want to get into the PT field, that’s why Im here with determination.

I moved to Sierra Vista, Arizona at the beginning of this year from China. I did a lot of research and consulted the PTA program at Pima in Tucson. However, I received a job offer as a preschool teacher in Seattle, so I moved there and started working in that position. Nevertheless, I have come to realize that my true passion lies in the field of physical therapy. That is why I am here with a strong determination to pursue a career in PT.

check grammar:Because I work with children, being a preschool teacher requires patience, energy, motivation and hard work. I think one of my strong strength is that i’m able to multitask, at my work I had to handle multiple situation at the same time, for example, one kid just had a potty accident, while another kid is crying over a toy, and another kid is asking me where is her book, I had to handle this situation at the same time,so I tell the third kid i have to change first kid’s cloth, I’ll help find your book after I change him, then I would tell the second kid, it’s ok, take a deep breath calm down, first,huge him and tell the first kid go to the bathroom and find his pants and socks, then I try to solve second kids problem offer him some solutions, then I would change first kids cloth

Because of my work with children, being a preschool teacher requires patience, energy, motivation, and hard work. One of my strong strengths is my ability to multitask. In my job, I often have to handle multiple situations simultaneously. For example, there might be one child who has had a potty accident, another child crying over a toy, and yet another child asking me where her book is. In these situations, I need to handle everything at the same time.

To manage this, I would first inform the third child that I have to change the first child's clothes and promise to help find her book after I'm done. Then, I would comfort the second child by telling them it's okay, encouraging them to take a deep breath and offering a hug. I would instruct the first child to go to the bathroom and find their pants and socks. After that, I would provide possible solutions to the second child and assist them further. Finally, I would proceed to change the first child's clothes.

Overall, my ability to multitask in a preschool environment has proven to be one of my key strengths.
what does physical therapy assistant do?

A Physical Therapist Assistant (PTA) works under the supervision of a Physical Therapist (PT) to provide rehabilitative care to patients. Their responsibilities include:

1. Assisting in patient evaluations: PTAs help PTs assess patients by gathering information on their medical history, observing their abilities, and performing specific tests or measurements.

2. Implementing treatment plans: Based on the PT's assessment, PTAs carry out treatment plans designed to improve patients' mobility, reduce pain, and restore function. This may involve therapeutic exercises, manual therapy techniques, electrical stimulation, or other modalities.

3. Monitoring patient progress: PTAs track and document patients' responses to treatment, noting any changes or improvements. This information helps PTs make necessary adjustments to the treatment plan.

4. Educating and instructing patients: PTAs educate patients and their families about their conditions, treatment procedures, and home exercises. They ensure that patients understand and correctly perform exercises or use assistive devices.

5. Managing equipment and assisting with modalities: PTAs assist with the setup and use of therapeutic equipment and modalities, such as therapeutic ultrasound, traction, or hot/cold packs.

6. Providing support and motivation: PTAs offer emotional support and encouragement to patients, helping them maintain motivation throughout their rehabilitation process.

7. Collaborating with the healthcare team: PTAs communicate and collaborate with other healthcare professionals, including PTs, physicians, nurses, and occupational therapists, to ensure comprehensive and coordinated patient care.

It's important to note that the scope of a PTA's practice and responsibilities may vary based on state regulations and the specific clinical setting in which they work. PTAs play a vital role in assisting PTs and providing hands-on care to help patients achieve their therapeutic goals.

check grammar:When I’m studying I like to make a chart documenting each units objectives, so it helps review the main knowledge I need to know for each unit, even in my class I made google docs document what’ve learned each week, so when parents ask me I can easily tell them what we’ve talked about in the class. I found it very effective .

When I am studying, I like to create a chart that documents the objectives for each unit. This not only helps me review the main knowledge I need to know for each unit but also allows me to easily communicate with parents. In my classes, I even maintain a Google Docs document where I record what we have learned each week. So, when parents ask me, I can readily inform them about the topics that have been covered in class. I have found this method to be highly effective.
